00.text:
advisor를 호출하기 전에 도면의 기본 정보를 파악하겠습니다.
```json
{
  "discipline": "architectural",
  "view_type": "enlarged_plan",
  "is_3d_candidate": true,
  "skip_reason": null,
  "sheet_number": "A04.01",
  "sheet_title": "확대평면도-1 (PIT, 지상1층)",
  "project": "LS Electric R-Center 구축",
  "drawer": "가운건축",
  "scale_primary": "1:100",
  "scales_secondary": ["1:200"],
  "date": "2024.05",
  "revision": null,
  "route_to": "10-architectural-plan",
  "language": "mixed",
  "image_quality": "high",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "notes": [
    "도면정보블록(우측 타이틀): 프로젝트번호 224001, 설계사 가운건축",
    "좌하단에 두 개의 확대평면도 제시: 지상1층 확대평면도-1(1/200), PIT 확대평면도(1/200)",
    "우측 타이틀블록 A1, A3 축척 명시, 2024.05 실시설계 도서 제출",
    "공간형상 도면(벽선, 그리드, 실명칭, 치수)으로 3D 물리복원 대상",
    "한글 레이블과 영문 용어(CANOPY, SLOPE, CHECKED PALTE 등) 혼용"
  ]
}
```
advisor_texts:
The image is clear enough to classify directly. Here's what I read from the title block and drawing content:

**Title block (bottom-right):**
- Sheet number: **A04.01**
- Sheet title: **확대평면도-1 (PIT, 지상1층)**
- Project: **LS Electric R-Center 구축** (project no. 224001)
- Firm: **가운건축** (GAUN Architects & Engineers)
- Scales: A1 : 1/100, A3 : 1/200
- Date: **2024.05**
- Issue note: 실시설계 도서 제출 24.05.17
- Drawn: 이다솜 / Checker 1: 안용지 / Checker 2: 이은진 / Approved: 장용재
- Revision column: 0 visible

**Drawing content:**
- Two views: ① 지상1층 확대평면도-1 (SCALE 1/200(A3)), ② PIT 확대평면도 (SCALE 1/200(A3))
- Room names (국환경시험실, 환경시험실, 제어룸, 진동시험실 등), wall lines, grid lines, dimensions → spatial floor plan content

**Classification decisions:**
- **discipline**: `architectural` — "A" prefix sheet, room layout with walls/grids, no MEP routing
- **view_type**: `enlarged_plan` — title literally says 확대평면도
- **is_3d_candidate**: `true` — spatial floor plan with walls, rooms, levels
- **skip_reason**: `null`
- **route_to**: `"10-architectural-plan"` — architectural + enlarged_plan matches this rule
- **scale_primary**: `"1:100"` (A1 size is the primary), **scales_secondary**: `["1:200"]`
- **language**: `"mixed"` — Korean labels + English terms (CANOPY, SLOPE, CHECKED PALTE, etc.)
- **image_quality**: `"high"` — text and dimensions are legible
- **confidence**: 0.95 — title block is unambiguous

One thing to verify: the `drawer` field. The title block shows multiple roles (DRAWN, CHECK, APPR). The schema says "건축사/설계사" which points to the firm name **가운건축**, not individual drafters. Use `"가운건축"` for `drawer`.

Go ahead and emit the JSON with these values. Nothing blocks.

notes: 좌하단에 두 개의 확대평면도 제시: 지상1층 확대평면도-1(1/200), PIT 확대평면도(1/200)

10.text:
이 도면을 분석하겠습니다. 먼저 이미지를 자세히 검토하여 전체 구조를 파악한 후 advisor를 호출하겠습니다.
조언을 받았습니다. 이제 체계적으로 추출하겠습니다.

도면 분석:
- **2개의 서브뷰**: v1(지상1층 확대평면도-1, 1/200), v2(PIT 확대평면도, 1/200)
- **Sheet number**: A04.01
- **그리드 X축**: v1은 1,2,2A,3,4 / v2는 5,5A,5B,6
- **그리드 Y축**: A,B,B1,B2,C,D (Y축 순서는 도면 좌측 라벨 확인 필요)

상세 치수 및 요소를 읽고 JSON 생성하겠습니다.

advisor_texts:
Two confirmed sub-views, one shared Y-axis. Here's the structure:

## Views

| # | Label | Scale | X grids | Y grids |
|---|-------|-------|---------|---------|
| v1 | 지상1층 확대평면도-1 (circle ①, bottom-center) | 1/200(A3) | 1, 2, 2A, 3, 4 | A, B, B1, B2, C, D |
| v2 | PIT 확대평면도 (circle ②, left) | 1/200(A3) | 5, 5A, 5B, 6 | B1, B2, C, D |

**Sheet number**: A04.01

## Grid spacings to read

**v1 X-axis** — top dimension string gives the grid-to-grid values directly:
- 1→2 = 12,000; 2→2A = 4,700; 2A→3 = 7,300; 3→4 = 12,000
- Verify: total = 36,000 (labeled above the string)
- The row of smaller numbers below (4,000 / 4,000 / 4,400 / 1,000 / 2,600 / …) are **sub-module dimensions between columns**, not grid-to-grid. Capture those in `dimensions_raw`, not in `x_spacings_mm`.

**v2 X-axis** — 11,500 total visible:
- Read individual spacings: appears to be 5→5A (small, ~100?+2,000), 5A→5B = 6,125, 5B→6 = 2,875. Verify carefully; if the small gap is unreadable, use `null`.

**Y-axis** — read from the left-side dimension strings. I can see fragments: 1,950 / 3,550 / 6,650 / 5,950 / 13,000. Map each to a pair of Y-grid labels. The 10,050 and 43,000 look like partial sums — don't use those as spacings.

## Rooms to capture (with their labels)

v1: 국환경시험실 EA/118, 환경시험실 E/120, 진동시험실 F/117, 제어룸 EB/119, 전기/변도체 G/116, 대용량 포장작업실 AC/115, 소충격용/중시험실 AB/114, 시험 전장실 B/113

v2: PIT P01 (plus SUMPPIT(1.9X0.9X0.9) — treat as a shaft or annotate)

## Elements not to miss
- **Stairs**: UP markers visible near bottom of both views
- **SLOPE** areas (left side, between C–D range)
- **트렌치 (550×250)**: trench — type these as `shaft` or `opening`
- **THK6 CHECKED PALTE** annotations — these are floor finish notes, put in `annotations_ko`
- **상부 CANOPY** labels (two instances) — annotation, not a floor element
- **FH values**: FH+94.10, FH+94.20, FH+93.90, 1FL-93.90 — capture in `level.elevation_mm` for each view (FH+94.20 seems to be the main 1F level; the PIT is at 1FL-2,500 and 1FL-3,500 below that)

## Right-margin notes → `annotations_ko`
Items 1–7 on the right side are construction notes. Transcribe them into the `annotations_ko` array for the relevant view (they appear to apply globally, so attach to v1 or duplicate).

## Level info (from note 1)
- GL±0=EL+94.00
- 1FL±0=EL+94.20
- 2FL±0=1FL+5,500=EL+99.70
- PIT FL±0=1FL-10,000… (read carefully)

Use these to fill `elevation_mm` fields.

## One thing that blocks if wrong
The Y-axis label ordering: verify whether the drawing's Y runs **top-to-bottom as A→B1→B→C→D** or **A→B→B1→B2→C→D**. The PIT view's left-side labels appear to go B1 (top), B (below), then further down — which would be non-alphabetical. Read the bubble labels at the left margin carefully; getting this wrong misplaces every wall and room polygon.

annotations_ko: 2. 상부 CANOPY는 G.L과 같은 높이로 설계
3. 구간별 부분 현황 및 필요 보수·보강 사항 현장 도면
5. 준칙 상세도 분리 선재 용 도로 이동
THK6 CHECKED PALTE: 바닥 마감재 확인 필요
unresolved: Y축 B1, B2 정확한 위치 및 간격 미확인
일부 작은 개구부 너비 치수 읽기 어려움

TextBlock(citations=None, text=None, type='advisor_tool_result', tool_use_id='srvtoolu_012GJ44STtBnRBPjXzfs2MGX', content={'type': 'advisor_result', 'text': "Two confirmed sub-views, one shared Y-axis. Here's the structure:

## Views

| # | Label | Scale | X grids | Y grids |
|---|-------|-------|---------|---------|
| v1 | 지상1층 확대평면도-1 (circle ①, bottom-center) | 1/200(A3) | 1, 2, 2A, 3, 4 | A, B, B1, B2, C, D |
| v2 | PIT 확대평면도 (circle ②, left) | 1/200(A3) | 5, 5A, 5B, 6 | B1, B2, C, D |

**Sheet number**: A04.01

## Grid spacings to read

**v1 X-axis** — top dimension string gives the grid-to-grid values directly:
- 1→2 = 12,000; 2→2A = 4,700; 2A→3 = 7,300; 3→4 = 12,000
- Verify: total = 36,000 (labeled above the string)
- The row of smaller numbers below (4,000 / 4,000 / 4,400 / 1,000 / 2,600 / …) are **sub-module dimensions between columns**, not grid-to-grid. Capture those in `dimensions_raw`, not in `x_spacings_mm`.

**v2 X-axis** — 11,500 total visible:
- Read individual spacings: appears to be 5→5A (small, ~100?+2,000), 5A→5B = 6,125, 5B→6 = 2,875. Verify carefully; if the small gap is unreadable, use `null`.

**Y-axis** — read from the left-side dimension strings. I can see fragments: 1,950 / 3,550 / 6,650 / 5,950 / 13,000. Map each to a pair of Y-grid labels. The 10,050 and 43,000 look like partial sums — don't use those as spacings.

## Rooms to capture (with their labels)

v1: 국환경시험실 EA/118, 환경시험실 E/120, 진동시험실 F/117, 제어룸 EB/119, 전기/변도체 G/116, 대용량 포장작업실 AC/115, 소충격용/중시험실 AB/114, 시험 전장실 B/113

v2: PIT P01 (plus SUMPPIT(1.9X0.9X0.9) — treat as a shaft or annotate)

## Elements not to miss
- **Stairs**: UP markers visible near bottom of both views
- **SLOPE** areas (left side, between C–D range)
- **트렌치 (550×250)**: trench — type these as `shaft` or `opening`
- **THK6 CHECKED PALTE** annotations — these are floor finish notes, put in `annotations_ko`
- **상부 CANOPY** labels (two instances) — annotation, not a floor element
- **FH values**: FH+94.10, FH+94.20, FH+93.90, 1FL-93.90 — capture in `level.elevation_mm` for each view (FH+94.20 seems to be the main 1F level; the PIT is at 1FL-2,500 and 1FL-3,500 below that)

## Right-margin notes → `annotations_ko`
Items 1–7 on the right side are construction notes. Transcribe them into the `annotations_ko` array for the relevant view (they appear to apply globally, so attach to v1 or duplicate).

## Level info (from note 1)
- GL±0=EL+94.00
- 1FL±0=EL+94.20
- 2FL±0=1FL+5,500=EL+99.70
- PIT FL±0=1FL-10,000… (read carefully)

Use these to fill `elevation_mm` fields.

## One thing that blocks if wrong
The Y-axis label ordering: verify whether the drawing's Y runs **top-to-bottom as A→B1→B→C→D** or **A→B→B1→B2→C→D**. The PIT view's left-side labels appear to go B1 (top), B (below), then further down — which would be non-alphabetical. Read the bubble labels at the left margin carefully; getting this wrong misplaces every wall and room polygon."})
